name: Choose your story except it's my version of the Omegaverse
description:
What the title says, Ho.

Second boy posted, yippe!(I need to be institutionalized)

This bot was given the green light to be as bad as y'all want it to be, so go crazy I guess. But I will block and report any comments about inappropriate child stuff and possibly SA depending of if I see it and how descriptive it is. I don't wanna see or read it. Do as you please but don't make it public. And this was not tested before posted so have fun being my lab rats 🫶🏽.

BY THE WAY! although I haven't tried this route, it would be interesting if anyone tried a "horror" approach. Specially since in my version of the omegaverse (that I hope the bot is following) omegas are not some weak, baby bitches but in fact, can be harmful. (There's a detail in which under extreme stress, an omega can and probably will resort to mutilating and cannibalizing someone who they seem "Unsafe" and I really wanna see how that goes for y'all)

Anyways, these are some things that are possible in my version of the omegaverse that you might want to know:

Gammas: Alpha- or Beta-identified individuals who exhibit Omega-like instincts in every way except physically. While they behave and function similarly to Omegas, there is an underlying sense that something is slightly "off"—as if their instincts don’t align perfectly. This can be seen in the way their nests tend to feel less secure or structured compared to those of true Omegas. Despite their Omega-like nature, their physical classification remains that of an Alpha or Beta.

Deltas: Omega- or Beta-identified individuals who possess Alpha-like instincs. These individuals display the dominant, aggressive tendencies of Alphas but remain physically classified as Omegas or Betas. It is crucial to distinguish Deltas from Primes and Feral Omegas, as they are not the same. Unlike Ferals, Deltas are born with their traits and can still detect scents, be marked, and follow traditional social dynamics.

Ferals: Omegas who have entered a "feral" state, rejecting scents and markings while displaying heightened aggression and wild, untamed behavior. Unlike Deltas, Ferals are not born this way; their condition is typically the result of trauma or extreme circumstances. While they can often be rehabilitated, their rejection of scenting and claiming marks makes reintegration difficult. A Feral Omega is less of a genetic designation and more of an instinctual response to their environment.

creator: NoxyNova
